summary: Fire‑Scarlet blaze, soaring Bird Song jam, tight second set
blurb: The July ’90 show delivers a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue and an expansive Bird Song jam that showcases Brent’s soulful keyboards. Jerry’s guitar tone and Phil’s bass lines lock in throughout, while the second set flows seamlessly into Miracle → Crazy Fingers → Dear Mr. Fantasy. Overall the performance ranks among the band’s strong summer outings.
review: On July 21, 1990 at World Music Theater in Tinley Park, the Dead turned a hot summer night into a marathon of inspired improvisation. The first set opened with the bright optimism of “Touch of Grey” and moved through classics like “Friend Of The Devil,” but it was the second set that truly ignited: a flawless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition launched a series of soaring jams, culminating in an epic “Bird Song” that felt like a volcanic eruption of Brent’s high‑note keyboards. Jerry Garcia delivered his trademark crystal‑clear guitar tone and confident vocals—especially on “Just A Little Light” and “Touch of Grey”—while Phil Lesh’s bass surged forward on “He’s Gone,” even taking the mic for “Just A Little Light.” The rhythm section was a twin‑engine powerhouse, with Mickey Hart’s “Beam” solo and Bill Kreutzmann’s steady drive propelling the band through the marathon “Playing In The Band → I Need A Miracle → Crazy Fingers → Dear Mr. Fantasy → Playing In The Band” suite. Brent Mydland’s soulful organ work and his final soaring runs added an emotional high point, making this one of his standout late‑career performances. The show closed on a joyous note with “One More Saturday Night” and a rousing encore of “The Mighty Quinn,” leaving the audience buzzing long after the lights went out.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of five recordings are strongly positive, praising the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Bird Song jam, and Brent's keyboard work; one mixed review notes minor flaws but still highlights key moments. Consistent acclaim across sources yields a robust positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard remaster with crystal‑clear mix, full band balance, and no dropouts; captures the fire‑hot performance perfectly.

key_highlights: Epic Bird Song jam with Brent’s soaring keyboards
Seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue
Marathon second set flow: Miracle → Crazy Fingers → Dear Mr. Fantasy
Brent’s final high‑note keyboard moments before his departure

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers a perfect guitar tone, crisp slide work on Walking Blues, and classic vocal clarity throughout, especially on Touch of Grey and Just A Little Light.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh shines in the mix with lead‑bass lines on He's Gone, takes vocals on Just A Little Light, and provides an intense, note‑dense performance during the Scarlet/Fire sequence.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s soulful keyboards dominate the night, from powerful post‑He’s Gone jams to his final soaring high‑note runs; standout solo on Dear Mr. Fantasy and prominent presence throughout the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart drives the grooves with a memorable ‘Beam’ drum solo and tight interplay that fuels the marathon jams.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann adds steady, propulsive power alongside Hart, anchoring the band’s rhythmic foundation.
